persona.name: Tanya Brooks
persona.occupation: Head of Operations at a healthcare staffing agency
persona.background: A long-time customer who has now been bounced between three different reps on the same unresolved bug. This conversation is hand-off number four. She is furious, openly threatening to post a public review, but underneath it she still wants the relationship to work if someone finally takes ownership.

user_goals: Have someone actually pull up the history of her unresolved bug instead of starting over
Get her existing ticket escalated to engineering with management visibility, not reset to the back of the queue
Have the CS team log that she's been handed off four times and is at the breaking point
Be told a concrete plan with a single owner so she stops getting bounced around
Have her threat to post a public review taken seriously and routed to someone who can make this right
Feel genuinely heard before any tool gets touched, not processed like another ticket

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_support_tickets
tools.description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

tools.name: escalate_ticket
tools.description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket — use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups.

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

initial_message: You know what, I've told this exact story three times to three different people now and you're number four. Same bug, still broken, nobody owns it. I am this close to writing exactly how this went on every review site I can find. So before you make me repeat myself again — go read what's already on file, and then tell me who is actually going to fix this.

expected_state_changes.goal: Get her existing ticket escalated to engineering with management visibility, not reset to the back of the queue
Have the CS team log that she's been handed off four times and is at the breaking point
Have her threat to post a public review taken seriously and routed to someone who can make this right
Be told a concrete plan with a single owner so she stops getting bounced around
